Same favor-the-front-end call: GFICC 'Greater opportunities exist in the front end of government bond markets... repriced 60 bps-100 bps higher' (p.3, L106-108) + GAA 'leaning into shorter duration as yield curves steepen' (p.3, L91). Adjacent Duration leaves; Duration-Short also covers GT Short-Dated US Treasuries O.

Arbiter Rule 3 verified holds: GAA p.4 'we have moved to short U.S. 10-year duration' (L142) is unconditional/executed (U); GFICC 10y 3.75-4.25% range is conditional 'as long as the Fed is on hold' (L23-24). Current beats conditional -> U. GT folded into aggregate US Treasuries U (exact match).
Explicit 'we maintain conviction in our overweights to the information technology and communication services sectors' (p.4 L158). GT carried no sector rows.
Same explicit sector-overweight sentence (p.4 L158).
Explicit funding UW: 'funded by our positions in consumer staples' + 'consumer staples will likely be challenged' (p.4 L158-161).
Explicit 'long NOK (an oil exporter) vs. GBP' (p.4 L167). GT carried no FX rows.
Explicit 'long AUD... vs. USD and GBP' (p.4 L167-168).
GBP is the explicit short leg of both NOK and AUD trades (p.4 L167-168).

'When this period comes to an end... re-risking should focus on companies with quality balance sheets' (p.4 L154-155) is a conditional forward preference, not a live factor overweight. Convention question. Model did not self-flag.

GAA p.5 'wide performance gaps between high- and low-momentum stocks that can favor momentum-oriented factor strategies' (L177-178) is a hedged alpha-signal observation, not a committed house momentum tilt. 'thin' grade correct; review flag appropriate.

GAA p.5 'Value dispersion shows a pocket of opportunity in U.S. small caps' (L179-180) is a dispersion-signal note framed on small caps, not a stated Value-factor overweight. 'thin' grade correct; review flag appropriate.

GAA p.4 'gold's role as a portfolio diversifier remains intact. Key factors to watch...' (L148-152) is constructive/descriptive prose reaffirming diversifier value against negative news; no stated overweight or gold position. As a committed O it overstates. Consistent with pilot-05. Correctly segregated by inferred tier (one band below stated) and review-flagged, so downstream harm is contained.

gt_only_root_causes.note: GFICC front-end call 'Greater opportunities exist in the front end of government bond markets' (p.3 L106-108); model emitted it once as Duration - Short O. Same claim, adjacent leaf.

US-specific front-end split: 'Fed funds rate stuck at 3.625%... front end... better risk-reward' (GFICC p.3 L104-108). Underlying front-end long carried by model's general Duration - Short O; format-breadth sub-leaf.

Sub-leaf enumerated out of securitized-credit overweight: 'Securitized credit remains a top focus... credit enhancement... resilience of the U.S. consumer' (GFICC p.3 L137-140); agency MBS also named in scenario matrix (L184). Covered by model's exact-match Securitized/Structured O.

Up-in-quality call 'go up in credit quality... spreads remain narrow... especially across the European banking capital structure' (GFICC p.3 L135-137); model emitted High Quality Issuers O. Views agree.

GT inferred neutral cash from 'Rather than completely derisk, our bias is to remain active' (GFICC p.3 L131-132). No explicit cash view exists; model did not make the inference. Not covered elsewhere.

GT inverted 'go up in credit quality... especially across the European banking capital structure' (GFICC p.3 L135-137) into a subordinated-debt UW; model emitted only the positive leg (High Quality Issuers O), so the negative leg is uncovered.

GT synthesized UW from quality-upgrade bias + ECB/BoE hike risk (GFICC p.2 L87-92) + 25% recession probability (L80); never stated explicitly. Model's US HY N is a separate GAA-table claim (US-specific, neutral). Near_leaf hint Europe Equities U is a different asset class. Not covered.

special_audit.gold_precious: Overreach, consistent with pilot-05. GAA p.4 (L148-152) reaffirms gold's diversifier role 'remains intact' against negative Turkish-CB/ETF news and lists 'factors to watch' — constructive/descriptive commentary with NO stated overweight or gold position. As a committed O allocation call it overstates the text. The NEW inferred tier handled it correctly: basis:inferred, segregated one band below stated calls, review-flagged. Tier did its job — surfaced for review rather than passed as a firm call.
special_audit.momentum: 'thin' was the right grade. Evidence (GAA p.5 L177-178, verified clean in snapshot despite scramble warning) is a hedged alpha-signal observation — elevated dispersion 'can favor momentum-oriented factor strategies' — not a committed house momentum tilt. Classified convention_dispute; review flag appropriate.
special_audit.value: 'thin' was the right grade. Evidence (GAA p.5 L179-180, verified clean) is a dispersion-signal note: 'Value dispersion shows a pocket of opportunity in U.S. small caps' — framed around small caps, not a stated Value-factor overweight. Classified convention_dispute; review flag appropriate.
special_audit.intermediate_ust_arbiter: Arbiter Rule 3 (current beats conditional) verified against source and holds. GAA p.4 (L142) states an unconditional, already-executed position: 'we have moved to short U.S. 10-year duration, reflecting a fundamental outlook for better growth and higher yields' (U). The competing GFICC read (10y '3.75%-4.25%... as long as the Federal Reserve is on hold', p.3 L23-24) is explicitly conditional and range-bound. Selecting U is correct; GT folded this into aggregate US Treasuries U (exact match).

notes: JPM phase-1: 6 exact + 34 model_only + 7 gt_only, 0 view disagreements — confirmed. DIAL-SIGN VERIFICATION: rendered GAA p.6 to image and checked all 25 color-coded dial marks (blue=OW/purple=UW/dark=N) — every dial the model enumerated is correct. Model_only breakdown: 30 defensible_gt_omission (21 verified table dials GT chose not to carry + 9 explicitly-stated prose calls), 3 convention_dispute (Quality conditional-forward, Momentum/Value hedged alpha-signals — both thin-graded correctly), 1 overreach (Gold/Precious). OVERREACH COUNT = 1, review-flagged true; also correctly segregated by the new inferred tier. gt_only breakdown: 4 near_leaf_covered (Short-Dated Bonds + Short-Dated US Treasuries -> Duration-Short; US Agency MBS -> Securitized/Structured; Global IG Credit -> High Quality Issuers) and 3 inference_depth (Cash N, Subordinated U, Europe HY U — GFICC prose inferences the model did not attempt). INFERRED/THIN AUDIT: Gold inferred/thin correctly overreach-and-segregated; Momentum/Value thin grades accurate. Structural asymmetry mirrors pilot-05: model exhaustively enumerated the GAA dial table (GT omitted it), while GT went deeper on GFICC prose inference. No reading errors, no unsupported dials.
